extractions.problem: The pathological mechanisms underlying metal fume fever, cardiovascular effects, and lung damage from inhaled metal oxide nanoparticles are not fully understood.
extractions.method: In vitro exposure of human alveolar epithelial cells, vascular endothelial cells, and whole blood to characterized gunshot smoke metal particles (0.1–50 μg/ml), measuring ROS, cytokines, and coagulation markers.
extractions.finding: Metal particles induced ROS and pro-inflammatory cytokines in lung cells, activated endothelial cells via non-ROS mechanisms, and triggered platelet activation and blood coagulation in whole blood, orchestrating thrombogenesis.
extractions.dataset: Human alveolar epithelial cells, vascular endothelial cells, whole blood; gunshot smoke particles
extractions.metric: ROS production, cytokine levels (IL-8, GM-CSF, IL-6), sICAM-1, sCD40L, PDGF-BB, VEGF-A

extractions.problem: Simulating in vitro chemical fate following acute exposure using computational approaches.
extractions.method: Virtual Cell Based Assay (VCBA) simulation model for chemical fate prediction.

extractions.problem: Understanding carcinoma reversion mechanisms through computational modeling.
extractions.method: Virtual cell modeling of carcinoma reversion.

extractions.problem: Developing novel Schiff base ligand-metal complexes as potential cholesterol lowering and anticancer agents.
extractions.method: Synthesis, characterization, antimicrobial testing, ADME profiling, and computational studies of Schiff base transition metal complexes.

extractions.problem: Simulating the biophysics of glioblastoma multiforme cell migration and oxygen field evolution.
extractions.method: Agent-based hybrid model combining cell biophysics with oxygen field dynamics.
extractions.finding: In collective migration, cluster survival prevails over cell mitosis regardless of group size, delaying necrotic foci formation and reducing oxygen consumption rate.

extractions.problem: Understanding the biomechanical behavior of malaria-infected red blood cells across multiple scales.
extractions.method: Multiscale computational modeling of malaria-infected red blood cells.

extractions.problem: Modeling and simulating an integrated energy system combining copper-chlorine cycle, molten carbonate fuel cell, and heat recovery.
extractions.method: MATLAB/SIMULINK simulation with PID controller for copper-chlorine cycle, molten carbonate fuel cell, regenerative steam cycle, and electric heater.
extractions.dataset: Copper-chlorine thermochemical cycle, molten carbonate fuel cell system

extractions.problem: Developing a generalized whole-cell model for wastewater-fed microbial fuel cells.
extractions.method: Whole-cell mathematical modeling of microbial fuel cell performance.

extractions.problem: Evaluating the best in vitro liver models including whole-cell systems and transporter function for toxicity testing.
extractions.method: Expert discussion and review of in vitro liver model systems.

extractions.problem: Designing minimal genomes is limited by lack of appropriate design tools and biological knowledge, requiring expensive experimental iterations.
extractions.method: Two minimal genome design algorithms (GAMA and Minesweeper) using whole-cell model simulations of Mycoplasma genitalium on supercomputers.
extractions.finding: Produced thousands of in silico minimal genomes by combining minimization algorithms with whole-cell model simulations, demonstrating feasibility of computational genome design.
extractions.dataset: Mycoplasma genitalium whole-cell model (28 cellular submodels)
extractions.result: 1000s of in silico minimal genomes generated

extractions.problem: Forming virtual cells in manufacturing using clustering approaches.
extractions.method: Fuzzy c-means clustering for virtual cell formation.

extractions.problem: Frequent handoffs and limited wireless backhaul capacity in ultradense 5G networks cause high packet loss rates and signaling overhead.
extractions.method: Machine learning approach using particle swarm optimization (PSO) with modified K-means algorithm and weighted distance parameter for flexible virtual cell design.
extractions.finding: Optimal virtual cell solutions found efficiently through considerably fewer iterations compared to exhaustive search, balancing packet loss rate and handoff overhead.
extractions.metric: Packet loss rate, handoff overhead, number of iterations
extractions.result: Considerably fewer iterations than exhaustive search

extractions.problem: Building a virtual cell requires comprehensive understanding of protein network dynamics, necessitating large-scale perturbation proteome data and intelligent computational models.
extractions.method: Neural ordinary differential equation-based foundation model (ProteinTalks) pretrained on >38 million perturbed protein measurements in breast cancer cell lines.
extractions.finding: ProteinTalks robustly predicts drug efficacy and synergy, identifies novel drug combinations, uncovers resistance-associated proteins, and enhances prognosis prediction when integrated with clinical data.
extractions.dataset: 38M+ perturbed protein measurements in breast cancer cell lines, patient-derived tumor xenografts, clinical patient data
extractions.result: Consistently improved predictive accuracy across various downstream tasks

extractions.problem: Simulating the motion and interactions of cells in whole blood requires geometric reconstruction of elastic surfaces in immersed boundary methods.
extractions.method: Immersed boundary method with parameter-free radial basis functions for high-order meshless parametric reconstruction of point clouds and elastic force computations.
extractions.finding: Red blood cells are crucial for understanding platelet motion; vessel wall geometry has negligible effect in presence of RBCs. A novel platelet 'unicycling' motion was discovered in 3D simulations.

extractions.problem: Understanding how initial concentration of low molecular weight molecules affects cellular noise during transport across the E. coli cell envelope.
extractions.method: Novel agent-based model simulating single-molecule transport across the cell envelope of E. coli.
extractions.finding: Cellular noise during molecule diffusion is concentration-dependent and size-independent.

extractions.problem: Simulating mammalian cell culture bioreactor processes using high-performance computational approaches.
extractions.method: Agent-based modeling of mammalian cell culture bioreactor.

extractions.problem: Understanding the utility of mathematical and computational tools in addressing questions of biological importance in NK cell biology and the immune system.
extractions.method: Survey and evaluation of mathematical and computational modeling approaches applied to NK cell biology.
extractions.finding: Quantitative tools are increasingly valuable for providing biological insights in NK cell biology, though ambivalence in the biology community persists.

extractions.problem: Understanding how Plasmodium falciparum infection and maturation affect the large deformation behavior of red blood cells.
extractions.method: 3D multiscale meshfree (element-free) method simulating optical tweezers experiments on malaria-infected red blood cells.
extractions.finding: Deformability of malaria-infected cells decreases significantly as infection progresses; simulation results agree well with experimental data.
extractions.result: Good agreement with experimental data; significant deformability decrease with infection progression

extractions.problem: Understanding how light-induced changes affect excitation energy transfer in photosystems of cyanobacteria, particularly high-light tolerance mechanisms.
extractions.method: Spectroscopic assessment of whole cells from Synechocystis sp. PCC 6803 and Synechococcus elongatus UTEX 2973 grown under high and low light conditions.
extractions.finding: Synechococcus 2973 employs an uncommon photoprotection strategy possibly involving zeaxanthin via an OCP homolog, downregulates phycobilisome antenna under high light, and has more tightly regulated PSII/PSI stoichiometric changes.
extractions.dataset: Synechocystis sp. PCC 6803, Synechococcus elongatus UTEX 2973
extractions.baseline: Low light growth condition; Synechocystis 6803 as comparison

extractions.problem: Integrating various cellular subsystems and predicting comprehensive cellular behaviors on a genome-wide scale remains a major challenge for whole-cell modeling.
extractions.method: Review of whole-cell modeling approaches including model construction, simulation, and analysis techniques for prokaryotes, yeasts, and eukaryotic cells.
extractions.finding: Whole-cell modeling has been applied to diverse organisms and applications including drug discovery and controlled differentiation, with extensions to multiscale and multicellular modeling.

extractions.problem: Exploring whether deterministic models can reproduce single-channel and whole-cell ionic currents traditionally modeled with stochastic Markov approaches.
extractions.method: Modified deterministic model based on Liebovitch and Toth applied to a voltage-dependent K+ channel, compared with two-state Markov model.
extractions.finding: The modified deterministic model reproduces both single-channel currents simulated by a two-state Markov model and whole-cell currents from a population of K+ channels.
extractions.baseline: Two-state Markov stochastic model (Liebovitch and Toth 1991)
extractions.result: Successfully reproduces both single-channel and whole-cell currents

extractions.problem: The mechanisms of force-dependent regulation of cell-substrate adhesions are unclear due to spatially heterogeneous distribution of endogenous forces within cells.
extractions.method: Micropatterning technique on square patterns with immunostaining and immunoblot analysis of paxillin phosphorylation in U2OS cells, combined with pharmacological force inhibition and a minimal mathematical model.
extractions.finding: Paxillin phosphorylation responds oppositely to mechanical forces at subcellular vs whole-cell levels; a minimal model reconciles this by showing negative local regulation combined with force-dependent protein assembly.
extractions.dataset: Human osteosarcoma U2OS cells on square micropatterns
extractions.result: Opposite mechano-response between subcellular and whole-cell levels

extractions.problem: Analyzing slow motion of couple stress fluid past a solid sphere in a virtual cell with slip effects.
extractions.method: Analytical/numerical modeling of couple stress fluid dynamics with slip boundary conditions in a virtual cell geometry.
extractions.dataset: Couple stress fluid, solid sphere in virtual cell

extractions.problem: Predicting chemical toxicity in 3D cell cultures for risk assessment, extending existing 2D models.
extractions.method: Extension of the 2D Virtual Cell Based Assay (VCBA) to a 3D spheroid model using quasi-diffusion process with concentric cell layers.
extractions.finding: The 3D VCBA model simulates close correlation between compound concentration influence and HepaRG 3D cell culture viability, showing promising agreement with in vitro data.
extractions.metric: Cell viability correlation with compound concentration
extractions.baseline: 2D VCBA model; empirical in vitro 3D spheroid measurements
extractions.result: Close correlation between simulated and in vitro viability data

extractions.problem: Forming virtual cells in manufacturing considering real-life production factors.
extractions.method: Flexible clustering using Kohonen self-organising map for virtual cell formation.

extractions.problem: Handling complex urban traffic behavior such as signalized intersections in cell transmission models.
extractions.method: Extended Urban Cell Transmission Model (UCTM) using agent-based concepts for turning, traffic signal, and lane-changing behavior modeling.
extractions.finding: The model replicates periodical queue generation/dissipation at signalized intersections and overall urban traffic phenomena adequately.
extractions.result: Adequately replicates urban traffic phenomena (validated with NGSIM)

extractions.problem: Understanding the role of regulatory T cells (Tregs) in preventing or slowing Type 1 Diabetes progression.
extractions.method: 2D agent-based model simulating immune response in mice, testing effects of Tregs from different sources and with different functions on tissue protection.
extractions.finding: A small number of Tregs can effectively protect islets by inhibiting effector T cell proliferation via DCs; Tregs added mid-response inhibit effector CD8+ T cell production and maintain β cell regeneration environment.
extractions.dataset: Simulated mouse pancreatic tissue immune response
extractions.metric: Islet protection, effector T cell proliferation inhibition
extractions.result: Small number of Tregs effectively protects islets

extractions.problem: Understanding smooth muscle cell migration induced by mechanical vibration.
extractions.method: Agent-based modelling of smooth muscle cell migration under mechanical vibration.

extractions.problem: Determining the relative contribution of free virus versus cell-to-cell transmission to hepatitis C virus dissemination in vitro.
extractions.method: Agent-based model explicitly incorporating distal (free-virus) and local (cell-to-cell) infection modes, validated with in vitro HCV data with and without neutralizing antibodies.
extractions.finding: Cell-to-cell infection accounts for 99% of infection events, yet blocking free-virus route causes 57% reduction at 72 hpi because free-virus provides access to distant uninfected areas enhancing cell-to-cell spread.
extractions.dataset: In vitro HCV infections (with/without free-virus neutralizing antibodies)
extractions.metric: Percentage of infection events by route, infection event reduction
extractions.result: Cell-to-cell: 99% (84–100% CI) of infections; free-virus block: 57% reduction at 72 hpi

extractions.problem: Generating whole-cell models requires high-resolution imaging of cellular ultrastructure.
extractions.method: Soft X-ray tomography for 3D imaging of whole cells to generate structural data for whole-cell models.

extractions.problem: Understanding the effects of priming with whole-cell Bordetella pertussis vaccine on immunity.
extractions.method: Mathematical modeling of whole-cell pertussis vaccine priming effects.

extractions.problem: Investigating whether the assumption that 28 independent modules can be simulated with a 1-second time step is valid for the M. genitalium whole-cell model.
extractions.method: Simulation of M. genitalium cell cycle using various simulation time step sizes, comparing results to the standard 1-second step.
extractions.metric: Cell cycle simulation results vs step size

extractions.problem: Developing a fiber-optic biosensor with bioluminescent whole-cell bioreporters for remote in-situ detection of BTEX compounds.
extractions.method: Mathematical model of optical element shape based on geometric optics, with MATLAB numerical simulation of ray transmission for different OE shapes; Pseudomonas putida TVA8 as bioreporter.
extractions.finding: Biosensor sensitivity depends on detected bioluminescence level, number of immobilized cells, and optical element transmission properties.
extractions.dataset: Pseudomonas putida TVA8 bioreporter; BTEX compounds (benzene, toluene, ethylbenzene, xylenes)
extractions.metric: Bioluminescence signal (~500 nm), ray transmission efficiency

extractions.problem: Whole-cell model simulations using stochastic methods are extremely time-consuming for the largest known biochemical reaction networks.
extractions.method: Parallel implementation of stochastic simulation algorithm (SSA) applied to a whole-cell reaction network.
extractions.finding: Parallel SSA implementation accelerates whole-cell model simulation development.

extractions.problem: Implications of material-level improvements on lithium-ion battery cell performance are hard to quantify, hindering accelerated battery development.
extractions.method: Bottom-up battery cell design model calculating performance parameters from design parameters and material characteristics in a functional database structure from material to cell level.
extractions.finding: The model provides accurate estimations for cell weight, capacity, and energy density, validated on two different battery cells.
extractions.metric: Weight, capacity, energy density estimation accuracy
extractions.result: Accurate estimations for weight, capacity, and energy density

extractions.problem: Whether AI can build a virtual cell by modeling life's smallest unit.
Computing osmotic pressure of dilute electrolyte solutions with charged macro-ions, accounting for shape anisotropy and surface charge distribution beyond mean-field approximations.
extractions.method: Extended virtual cell model combining coarse-grained Monte Carlo simulations (one-body and two-body) with all-atom molecular dynamics, using spherical virtual subspace for protein number density.
extractions.finding: Protein concentration-dependent osmotic pressures can be obtained by confining counterions in a virtual spherical subspace; specific interaction parameters enable study of ion-specific effects with a single protein molecule.
extractions.result: Validated upper limit for neglecting macro-ion correlations; extended to all-atom MD

extractions.problem: Studying morphological variation in endothelial cell populations using computational modeling.
extractions.method: Virtual-cell model of endothelial cell morphological variation.

extractions.problem: Existing GRN inference methods rely on static models that fail to capture dynamic regulatory changes during cell differentiation, limiting virtual cell simulation.
extractions.method: CellProphet: interpretable AI model integrating temporal causality with transformer self-attention to infer dynamic gene regulatory networks with time-lagged TF-target dependencies.
extractions.finding: CellProphet achieves superior performance across all evaluation metrics on 7 differentiation datasets, identifies known and novel TFs, and accurately predicts cell fate transitions following in silico perturbation of Gata1 and Spi1.
extractions.dataset: 7 differentiation datasets; mouse embryonic stem cells; mouse hematopoietic differentiation
extractions.metric: GRN inference evaluation metrics (all), TF sensitivity
extractions.result: Superior performance in all evaluation metrics across 7 datasets

extractions.problem: Extensive crosstalk between programmed cell death pathways (apoptosis, pyroptosis, necroptosis) creates redundancies that challenge therapeutic control of cytokine storm.
extractions.method: Agent-based model (PCDABM) in NetLogo simulating activation of PCDPs by IAV, EPEC, and Salmonella enterica, with silencing experiments and simulated anti-TNF/anti-IL-1 therapies.
extractions.finding: PCDP redundancies maintain protection against diverse pathogens but challenge immunomodulation; anti-TNF and anti-IL-1 simulations did not reduce aggregate inflammation-generated system damage.
extractions.dataset: Simulated infections: influenza A virus (IAV), enteropathic E. coli (EPEC), Salmonella enterica (SE)
extractions.metric: Microbial clearance, aggregated inflammation-generated system damage
extractions.baseline: No PCDP silencing; no therapeutic intervention
extractions.result: Anti-TNF and anti-IL-1 did not reduce aggregate system damage

extractions.problem: Evaluating cost-effectiveness in the manufacture of allogeneic human induced pluripotent cells.
extractions.method: Agent-based model for cost-effectiveness analysis of hiPSC manufacturing in Japan.

extractions.problem: Designing steroid-based hydrazones as potential anabolic agents with computational and biological validation.
extractions.method: Design, synthesis, computational studies, and biological evaluation of steroid-based hydrazones.

extractions.problem: Agent-based models with deformable membranes in 3D environments face computational bottlenecks in how agents perceive their surroundings.
extractions.method: GPU algorithm (OpenCL/CUDA) enabling agents to integrate signals scattered on their virtual membranes in 3D agent-based simulations.
extractions.finding: The GPU algorithm provides significant speed improvements for membrane signal integration in current GPU devices.
extractions.dataset: Virtual cells with deformable membranes (up to millions of agents)

extractions.problem: Existing cell simulations lack fidelity and real-time interactivity for illustrating structures and processes within biological cells.
extractions.method: Eukaryo: 3D interactive agent-based simulation of a eukaryotic cell in a game engine with visual scripting, combining agent-based and mathematical modeling in VR.
extractions.finding: Eukaryo offers superior representations of cellular architecture, components, and dynamics compared to traditional media like illustrations and videos.
extractions.dataset: Eukaryotic cell (gene expression, energy production, waste removal)
extractions.result: Superior representations of cellular architecture and dynamics

extractions.problem: Modeling the budding yeast cell cycle regulatory network.
extractions.method: Agent-based model of the budding yeast cell cycle regulatory network.
